content_en:
Al-Junaid?’ said, "Whoever strives hard against his lusts in Our Cause by means of repentance, We will guide them to paths of sincerity. We will never be able to fight our enemy in the outside except when we fight our enemies within ourselves. Whoever gains victory over himself will gain victory over his enemies and whoever is defeated within himself will be defeated by his enemies.

Chapter: Enmity between the Mind and Imagination

Allah, the Exalted placed enmity between Satan and the Angels, the mind and the imagination, and the soul prone to evil and the heart. Allah combined all these forms of enmity within man and provided every party with supporters. The war between the two parties will continue until one captures the other and subdues it. If the hearts, the minds, and the angels win, there will be happiness, pleasure, joy, relief, and victory. However, if the soul, imagination, and Satan win, there will be

27 Al-Junaid bin Muhammad bin Al-Junaid Al-Baghdady Al-Khazzaz. His surname was Abu Al-Qasim. He was Sufi from scholars of religion. He was born and brought up in Baghdad and known as Al-Khazz4z because he used to work in Al-Khaz, which is silk. He was the first to talk about theology in Baghdad. Scholars considered him a sheikh as he adjusted his principles according to Qur'an and Sunnah. Among his letters is Dawa’ Al-Arwah. He passed away (may Allah be merciful to him) in 297 A.H. in Baghdad. See his biography in Al-Alam, vol.2, p.141. See Al-Bidayyah wan-Nihayah, vol.11,

sadness, worry, distress, and suppression of all that is good. What do you think about the king (that is you) that was captured by his enemy who dethroned, captured, imprisoned, and prevented him from using his treasures, supplies, and slaves and took them over. In spite of all this, this king did not move to take revenge or ask for help. Above this king, there is another king that is Almighty, the Conqueror, and the most Honored, so he should ask Him. If you ask His help He will help you, if you ask for His support He will support you, if you ask for His protection He will take revenge for you, and if you run to Him, He will give you authority over your enemy and subdue him. If this captured king (human being) said that his enemy (his soul, imagination, and Satan) tied him strongly and prevented him from rising, running to You (Allah), and walking to Your door, He, the Almighty just has to send some soldiers to untie him and take him out of his prison, then he can come to His door, otherwise he would never be able to untie himself.

If he said this in order to refute the Mighty King, His message, and accept his state with his enemy, this mighty king (Allah) will leave him to whoever he chose to be his friend.

However, if he said this because he really needs Him, shows his weakness, and humiliation, and shows that he is too weak to walk to Allah or untie himself, it would be a blessing from Allah to support him. If Allah helps him He would bestow blessings on him and if He did not, He would not be unjust to him even if he had praised Him earlier. His wisdom requires that. When a human knows that the prison is that of Allah and the enemy is His servant, who has no power to do anything good nor bad, then armies of help will come to him.

The highest ranks of intention in seeking knowledge is seeking knowledge of the Qur'an and Sunnah and knowledge of the laws of Allah. The meanest intentions are those seeking the

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 95

answers to hypothetical questions, that is neither divine nor real, or those who are hesitant and follow the words of others and do not have any intention to know what is right. It is rare if any of those obtain benefit from gaining knowledge.

The highest ranks of intention concerning ‘will’ is that intention should be attached to love for Allah and seeking His pleasure. The lowest is when one’s intention is based on human aims against the aim of Allah. That is to say that the person worships Allah according to his aim without seeking the aim of Allah.

Corrupt scholars sit in front of the door to Paradise calling people to it through some words, while they are doing deeds that lead to Hellfire. Whenever their words call people to Paradise, their deeds do not conform with their words. If such words were true they would have been followed by them. They seem to be leaders while they are really highwaymen.

If Allah alone is your aim, then all favor and honor will be yours but if you aim at what you will gain from Him, then favor may not come to you as it is in His Hands. If your aim was favor, which you would gain from Allah, then it would not come to you. This is because it is in His Hands and a Deed of His Deeds. If you draw nearer from Allah, all favor would be yours. If favor was your aim, it would not come to you. If you knew Allah and tasted the pleasure of being near Him and then you degraded yourself to seeking favor, Allah would prevent you from gaining it as a punishment. This way, you lost being near Allah and gaining favor.

A Precious Gem: The Messenger's Victory

When the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) tasted victory after being constrained by the enemy, and his army enjoyed victory, and he became eminent, all mankind was

divided into three categories: those who believed in him, those who made peace with him, and those who were fearful of him.

When he set the best examples of endurance, to which Allah says,

which means, "Therefore be patient (O Muhammad) as did the Messengers of strong will." (Al-Ahgaf, 46:35), the outcome was fruitful, to which Allah says,

which means, "and for the prohibited things, there is the Law of Equality (Qisas)." (Al-Baqarah, 2:194)

Hence, he conquered Mecca in an unprecedented way, surrounded by the MuhdajirGin (the immigrants) and the Ansar (the supporters). He was also surrounded by various ranks of Companions, while Jibril (Gabriel) was ascending and descending in between the heavens and the earth. Allah made no one conquer His sanctuary except His Messenger.

The Messenger (peace be upon him) compared the day of conquering Mecca and the day in which Allah says,

4b. Oh Bi als I ag ys al Gy 5g >

which means, "And (remember) when the disbelievers plotted against you (O Muhammad) to imprison you, or to kill you, or to get you out." (Al-Anfal, 8:30)

to when the disbelievers drove him out the second of the two, he entered Mecca lowering his chin to his saddle, submitting and complying to the orders of Allah, Who had granted him

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 97

victory; he was the one to whom mankind raised their heads, and to whom kings surrendered.

He entered Mecca victorious, supported and a conqueror, and Bilal mounted the Ka'bah, after having been tortured in its precincts. In a high voice, he recited the Adhan (the call for prayer), where all tribes came answering him from every place, entering the religion of Allah in crowds, while before they used to come individually.

When the Messenger (peace be upon him) ascended the pillar of glory, from which he never descended, all kings surrendered to him, and amongst them were those who granted him rulership in their countries, and others asked for peace and reconciliation, and those who consented to pay tribute, however, the fourth category gathered to be ready for war, as they were unaware of the fact that they were just gathering the booty and the prisoners of war to him.

Thus, when the Messenger's victory became obvious, along with conveying his message, and fulfilling his mission. Allah revealed to him,

£2 Nae Gel Sil Shag cp Gs ete Ghar He ae

which means, " Verily, We have given you (O Muhammad) a manifest victory, that Allah may forgive you your sins of the past and the future, and complete His Favour on you, and guide you on the Straight Path; And that Allah may help you with strong help." (Al-Fath, 48: 1-3)

And Allah also revealed to him,

EP EGM yo g DL plahy p Sally SIE ys

98 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

which means, "When comes the Help of Allah (to you, O Muhammad) against your enemies) and the conquest (of Mecca), And you see that the people enter Allah's religion (Islam) in crowds." (Al-Fath, 110: 1-2)

After revealing these verses, the Angel of death descended upon the Messenger (peace be upon him) enabling him to choose between his rank in this worldly life and meeting his Lord, to which he chose the meeting with his Lord, for which he was longing. On that day, paradise was adorned for the honorable soul (of Muhammad), more than the celebrations for any king on his arrival to his homeland.

ff the throne of the Beneficent shook due to the death of one of the Messenger's followers and rejoicing at his arrival, then what would be the case with the death of the best of all creatures. O you who are associated with this trivial worldly life, and are standing by another door, surely you will discover which secret will be yours on the day of gathering, in which Allah says,

which means, "The Day when all the secrets (deeds, prayers, fasting, etc.) will be examined (as to their truth)." (At-Tariq, 86:9)

Chapter: The Vanity of False Hopes

O you who are deceived by false hopes! Satan was cursed and descended from the glorious abode by neglecting one prostration he was ordered to perform. Adam descended from Paradise because of a mouthful he ate. The criminal was separated by a handful of blood. Moreover, the adulterer is supposed to be killed in the most awful way because he committed an unlawful act. Flogging is enjoined on him who says a word of slander, or drinks a drop of liquor. He (Allah) ordered that one of your hands should be cut in case theft is

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 99

committed, so do not fee! secure, for He might cast you into Hellfire because of only one sin.

Allah, the Almighty says,

which means, "And He (Allah) feared not the consequences thereof." (Ash-Shams, 91:15)
content_id:
Al-Junaid berkata, "Barangsiapa yang bersungguh-sungguh melawan hawa nafsunya di Jalan Kami melalui taubat, niscaya Kami akan membimbing mereka ke jalan-jalan keikhlasan. Kita tidak akan pernah mampu memerangi musuh di luar kecuali jika kita memerangi musuh-musuh di dalam diri kita. Barangsiapa yang meraih kemenangan atas dirinya sendiri, ia akan meraih kemenangan atas musuh-musuhnya, dan barangsiapa yang dikalahkan dalam dirinya sendiri, ia akan dikalahkan oleh musuh-musuhnya.

Bab: Permusuhan antara Akal dan Imajinasi

Allah Yang Maha Tinggi menciptakan permusuhan antara Setan dan para Malaikat, antara akal dan imajinasi, serta antara jiwa yang condong kepada kejahatan dan hati. Allah menggabungkan semua bentuk permusuhan ini dalam diri manusia dan memberikan setiap pihak pendukungnya. Perang antara dua pihak ini akan terus berlangsung hingga salah satunya menguasai dan menaklukkan yang lain. Jika hati, akal, dan para Malaikat menang, maka akan ada kebahagiaan, kesenangan, kegembiraan, ketenangan, dan kemenangan. Namun, jika jiwa, imajinasi, dan Setan menang, maka akan ada

27 Al-Junaid bin Muhammad bin Al-Junaid Al-Baghdady Al-Khazzaz. Kunyahnya adalah Abu Al-Qasim. Ia adalah seorang Sufi dari kalangan ulama agama. Ia lahir dan dibesarkan di Baghdad dan dikenal sebagai Al-Khazzaz karena bekerja di bidang Al-Khaz, yaitu sutra. Ia adalah orang pertama yang berbicara tentang ilmu kalam di Baghdad. Para ulama menganggapnya sebagai seorang syaikh karena ia menyesuaikan prinsip-prinsipnya dengan al-Qur'an dan Sunnah. Di antara surat-suratnya adalah Dawa' Al-Arwah. Ia wafat (semoga Allah merahmatinya) pada tahun 297 H di Baghdad. Lihat biografinya dalam Al-Alam, jil.2, hal.141. Lihat Al-Bidayyah wan-Nihayah, jil.11,

kesedihan, kekhawatiran, kesulitan, dan tertutupnya segala kebaikan. Apa pendapatmu tentang seorang raja (yaitu dirimu) yang ditawan oleh musuhnya, yang digulingkan, ditangkap, dipenjarakan, dan dihalangi dari menggunakan perbendaharaan, perbekalan, serta para hambanya, lalu semuanya diambil alih. Meskipun demikian, sang raja ini tidak bergerak untuk membalas dendam atau meminta pertolongan. Di atas raja ini, ada Raja yang lain, yaitu Allah Yang Maha Kuasa, Maha Perkasa, dan Maha Mulia, maka hendaklah ia memohon kepada-Nya. Jika engkau meminta pertolongan-Nya, Dia akan menolongmu; jika engkau meminta dukungan-Nya, Dia akan mendukungmu; jika engkau meminta perlindungan-Nya, Dia akan membalaskan dendammu; dan jika engkau berlari kepada-Nya, Dia akan memberikan kekuasaan atasmu terhadap musuhmu dan menaklukkannya. Jika raja yang tertawan ini (manusia) berkata bahwa musuhnya (hawa nafsu, imajinasi, dan Setan) telah membelenggunya dengan kuat dan menghalanginya untuk bangkit, berlari kepada-Mu (Allah), dan berjalan menuju pintu-Mu, maka Dia Yang Maha Kuasa tinggal mengirimkan beberapa tentara untuk melepaskan belenggunya dan membawanya keluar dari penjaranya, sehingga ia dapat datang ke pintu-Nya, karena jika tidak, ia tidak akan pernah mampu melepaskan dirinya sendiri.

Jika ia mengucapkan hal ini untuk menentang Raja Yang Maha Agung, pesan-Nya, dan menerima keadaannya bersama musuhnya, maka Raja yang Maha Agung ini (Allah) akan membiarkannya bersama siapa pun yang ia pilih sebagai temannya.

Namun, jika ia mengucapkan hal ini karena ia benar-benar membutuhkan-Nya, menampakkan kelemahannya dan kehinaannya, serta menunjukkan bahwa ia terlalu lemah untuk berjalan menuju Allah atau melepaskan belenggunya sendiri, maka itu merupakan karunia dari Allah untuk menolongnya. Jika Allah menolongnya, Dia akan melimpahkan berkah kepadanya, dan jika Dia tidak menolongnya, Dia tidaklah berlaku zalim kepadanya, meskipun ia telah memuji-Nya sebelumnya. Hikmah-Nya menghendaki demikian. Ketika seorang manusia mengetahui bahwa penjara itu milik Allah dan musuh itu adalah hamba-Nya, yang tidak memiliki daya untuk melakukan kebaikan maupun keburukan sedikit pun, maka pasukan-pasukan pertolongan akan datang kepadanya.

Tingkatan niat tertinggi dalam menuntut ilmu adalah menuntut ilmu al-Qur'an dan Sunnah serta ilmu tentang hukum-hukum Allah. Niat yang paling rendah adalah mereka yang mencari

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 95

jawaban atas pertanyaan-pertanyaan hipotetis yang tidak bersifat ilahiah maupun nyata, atau mereka yang ragu-ragu dan mengikuti perkataan orang lain tanpa memiliki niat untuk mengetahui kebenaran. Jarang sekali ada di antara mereka yang memperoleh manfaat dari menuntut ilmu.

Tingkatan niat tertinggi berkaitan dengan 'kehendak' adalah bahwa niat harus terikat pada kecintaan kepada Allah dan mencari ridha-Nya. Yang terendah adalah ketika niat seseorang didasarkan pada tujuan-tujuan manusiawi yang bertentangan dengan tujuan Allah. Artinya, orang tersebut beribadah kepada Allah sesuai dengan tujuannya sendiri tanpa mencari tujuan Allah.

Ulama-ulama yang rusak duduk di depan pintu Surga menyeru manusia kepadanya melalui beberapa perkataan, sementara mereka melakukan amal-amal yang mengarah ke Neraka. Setiap kali perkataan mereka menyeru manusia ke Surga, amal-amal mereka tidak sesuai dengan perkataan mereka. Seandainya perkataan-perkataan tersebut benar, niscaya mereka sendiri telah mengikutinya. Mereka tampak seperti pemimpin, padahal sesungguhnya mereka adalah perampok di jalan.

Jika Allah semata yang menjadi tujuanmu, maka segala karunia dan kehormatan akan menjadi milikmu. Namun jika tujuanmu adalah apa yang akan engkau peroleh dari-Nya, maka karunia itu mungkin tidak akan datang kepadamu karena ia berada di Tangan-Nya. Jika tujuanmu adalah karunia yang akan engkau peroleh dari Allah, maka karunia itu tidak akan datang kepadamu. Sebab karunia itu berada di Tangan-Nya dan merupakan salah satu Perbuatan-Nya. Jika engkau mendekatkan diri kepada Allah, segala karunia akan menjadi milikmu. Jika karunia yang menjadi tujuanmu, maka ia tidak akan datang kepadamu. Jika engkau telah mengenal Allah dan merasakan kelezatan kedekatan dengan-Nya, kemudian engkau merendahkan dirimu dengan mencari karunia, Allah akan menghalangimu dari mendapatkannya sebagai hukuman. Dengan demikian, engkau kehilangan kedekatan dengan Allah sekaligus kehilangan karunia.

Permata Berharga: Kemenangan Rasulullah

Ketika Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) merasakan kemenangan setelah dikepung oleh musuh, dan pasukannya menikmati kemenangan, serta beliau menjadi mulia, seluruh umat manusia

terbagi menjadi tiga golongan: mereka yang beriman kepada beliau, mereka yang berdamai dengan beliau, dan mereka yang merasa takut kepada beliau.

Ketika beliau menampilkan contoh terbaik dari kesabaran, yang Allah firmankan,

yang artinya, "Maka bersabarlah engkau (wahai Muhammad) sebagaimana para rasul yang memiliki keteguhan hati telah bersabar." (Al-Ahqaf, 46:35), hasilnya sangat membuahkan, sebagaimana yang Allah firmankan,

yang artinya, "dan bagi hal-hal yang terlarang, berlaku hukum Qisas." (Al-Baqarah, 2:194)

Maka beliau pun menaklukkan Mekah dengan cara yang belum pernah terjadi sebelumnya, dikelilingi oleh para Muhajirin (kaum yang berhijrah) dan Anshar (kaum yang membantu). Beliau juga dikelilingi oleh berbagai tingkatan para Sahabat, sementara Jibril (Malaikat Jibril) naik turun antara langit dan bumi. Allah tidak menjadikan seorang pun menaklukkan tanah suci-Nya kecuali Rasul-Nya.

Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) membandingkan hari penaklukan Mekah dan hari ketika Allah berfirman,

4b. Oh Bi als I ag ys al Gy 5g >

yang artinya, "Dan (ingatlah) ketika orang-orang kafir merencanakan tipu daya terhadapmu (wahai Muhammad) untuk memenjaramu, atau membunuhmu, atau mengusirmu." (Al-Anfal, 8:30)

dengan saat ketika orang-orang kafir mengusirnya sebagai yang kedua dari dua orang, beliau memasuki Mekah dengan menundukkan dagunya ke atas pelananya, tunduk dan patuh terhadap perintah-perintah Allah, yang telah menganugerahkan kepadanya

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 97

kemenangan; beliaulah orang yang kepadanya umat manusia mengangkat kepala mereka, dan kepadanya para raja menyerah.

Beliau memasuki Mekah sebagai pemenang, didukung dan sebagai penakluk, sementara Bilal naik ke atas Ka'bah, setelah sebelumnya pernah disiksa di sekitarnya. Dengan suara yang lantang, ia mengumandangkan Adzan (seruan untuk salah), di mana seluruh kabilah datang menyambutnya dari segala penjuru, memasuki agama Allah secara berbondong-bondong, padahal sebelumnya mereka biasa datang secara sendiri-sendiri.

Ketika Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) naik ke puncak kemuliaan, yang darinya beliau tidak pernah turun, semua raja menyerah kepadanya, dan di antara mereka ada yang menganugerahkan kekuasaan kepadanya di negeri-negeri mereka, yang lain meminta perdamaian dan rekonsiliasi, dan ada pula yang setuju untuk membayar jizyah. Namun, golongan keempat berkumpul bersiap untuk berperang, karena mereka tidak menyadari bahwa sesungguhnya mereka hanya sedang mengumpulkan ghanimah dan tawanan perang untuknya.

Demikianlah, ketika kemenangan Rasulullah menjadi nyata, beserta penyampaian risalahnya dan pelaksanaan misinya, Allah mewahyukan kepadanya,

£2 Nae Gel Sil Shag cp Gs ete Ghar He ae

yang artinya, "Sesungguhnya Kami telah memberikan kepadamu (wahai Muhammad) kemenangan yang nyata, agar Allah mengampuni dosamu yang telah lalu dan yang akan datang, serta menyempurnakan nikmat-Nya atasmu, dan membimbingmu ke jalan yang lurus; dan agar Allah menolongmu dengan pertolongan yang kuat." (Al-Fath, 48:1-3)

Dan Allah juga mewahyukan kepadanya,

EP EGM yo g DL plahy p Sally SIE ys

98 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

yang artinya, "Apabila telah datang pertolongan Allah (kepadamu, wahai Muhammad atas musuh-musuhmu) dan kemenangan (atas Mekah), Dan engkau melihat manusia masuk agama Allah (Islam) secara berbondong-bondong." (Al-Fath, 110:1-2)

Setelah ayat-ayat ini diturunkan, Malaikat maut turun kepada Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) memberinya pilihan antara kedudukannya dalam kehidupan dunia ini dan pertemuan dengan Tuhannya, dan beliau memilih pertemuan dengan Tuhannya, yang telah lama beliau rindukan. Pada hari itu, Surga dihias untuk ruh yang mulia (Muhammad), melebihi perayaan bagi raja mana pun saat tiba di tanah airnya.

Jika Arsy Ar-Rahman berguncang karena kematian salah seorang pengikut Rasul dan bersuka cita atas kedatangannya, maka bagaimana halnya dengan kematian makhluk terbaik dari seluruh ciptaan. Wahai engkau yang terikat dengan kehidupan dunia yang fana ini, dan berdiri di depan pintu yang lain, sungguh engkau akan mengetahui rahasia apa yang akan menjadi milikmu pada hari pengumpulan, di mana Allah berfirman,

yang artinya, "Pada hari ketika segala rahasia (amal perbuatan, salah, puasa, dan sebagainya) akan diperiksa (kebenaran dan keikhlasannya)." (At-Tariq, 86:9)

Bab: Kesia-siaan Harapan Palsu

Wahai engkau yang tertipu oleh harapan palsu! Iblis dilaknat dan diturunkan dari tempat tinggal yang mulia karena mengabaikan satu sujud yang diperintahkan kepadanya. Adam diturunkan dari Surga karena sesuap makanan yang dimakannya. Si penjahat terpisah karena segenggam darah. Lebih dari itu, pezina sudah seharusnya dibunuh dengan cara yang paling mengerikan karena ia melakukan perbuatan yang haram. Hukum cambuk dijatuhkan kepada orang yang mengucapkan sepatah kata fitnah, atau meminum setetes minuman keras. Dia (Allah) memerintahkan agar salah satu tanganmu dipotong apabila pencurian

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 99

dilakukan, maka janganlah merasa aman, karena Dia mungkin melemparkanmu ke dalam Neraka hanya karena satu dosa.

Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Dan Dia (Allah) tidak takut akan akibat dari perbuatan-Nya itu." (Ash-Shams, 91:15)
content_ar:
Al-Junaid?’ said, "Whoever strives hard against his lusts in Our Cause by means of repentance, We will guide them to paths of sincerity. We will never be able to fight our enemy in the outside except when we fight our enemies within ourselves. Whoever gains victory over himself will gain victory over his enemies and whoever is defeated within himself will be defeated by his enemies.

Chapter: Enmity between the Mind and Imagination

الله, the Exalted placed enmity between Satan and the Angels, the mind and the imagination, and the soul prone to evil and the heart. الله combined all these forms of enmity within man and provided every party with supporters. The war between the two parties will continue until one captures the other and subdues it. If the hearts, the minds, and the angels win, there will be happiness, pleasure, joy, relief, and victory. However, if the soul, imagination, and Satan win, there will be

27 Al-Junaid bin Muhammad bin Al-Junaid Al-Baghdady Al-Khazzaz. His surname was Abu Al-Qasim. He was Sufi from scholars of religion. He was born and brought up in Baghdad and known as Al-Khazz4z because he used to work in Al-Khaz, which is silk. He was the first to talk about theology in Baghdad. Scholars considered him a sheikh as he adjusted his principles according to Qur'an and Sunnah. Among his letters is Dawa’ Al-Arwah. He passed away (may الله be merciful to him) in 297 A.H. in Baghdad. See his biography in Al-Alam, vol.2, p.141. See Al-Bidayyah wan-Nihayah, vol.11,

sadness, worry, distress, and suppression of all that is good. What do you think about the king (that is you) that was captured by his enemy who dethroned, captured, imprisoned, and prevented him from using his treasures, supplies, and slaves and took them over. In spite of all this, this king did not move to take revenge or ask for help. Above this king, there is another king that is Almighty, the Conqueror, and the most Honored, so he should ask Him. If you ask His help He will help you, if you ask for His support He will support you, if you ask for His protection He will take revenge for you, and if you run to Him, He will give you authority over your enemy and subdue him. If this captured king (human being) said that his enemy (his soul, imagination, and Satan) tied him strongly and prevented him from rising, running to You (الله), and walking to Your door, He, the Almighty just has to send some soldiers to untie him and take him out of his prison, then he can come to His door, otherwise he would never be able to untie himself.

If he said this in order to refute the Mighty King, His message, and accept his state with his enemy, this mighty king (الله) will leave him to whoever he chose to be his friend.

However, if he said this because he really needs Him, shows his weakness, and humiliation, and shows that he is too weak to walk to الله or untie himself, it would be a blessing from الله to support him. If الله help
